title: Trisetum forskaolii
taxonomy: Trisetum forskaolii is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Trisetum, which comprises several species of grasses. The genus Trisetum is further classified within the subfamily Pooideae, which contains a large number of cool-season grasses. Trisetum forskaolii is native to Africa and is found in various habitats, including grasslands and savannas. The species is often characterized by its distinctive growth habit and leaf morphology.

intro: Trisetum forskaolii, also known as Forskaol's hair grass or Forskål's oat grass, is a perennial grass species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum forskaolii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trisetum forskaolii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules to harness its medicinal benefits.
